what_it_does: Defines a standalone design-system preview card showing a sidebar navigation convention that uses colour emoji as nav icons, with a short explanatory blurb and a 12-item grid of labelled nav examples.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment with group Brand, name Sidebar emoji convention, subtitle Colour emoji as nav icons — deliberate brand move, and viewport 700x240.
Links to _card.css and relies on design tokens such as --fg-secondary, --bg-surface, --border-faint, --r-md, and --font-body.
Renders a paragraph stating that colour emoji nav icons are a deliberate platform sidebar brand convention.
Displays 12 navigation items in a six-column CSS grid.
Marks Gallery as active using a dashed 1.5px border.
Uses literal emoji glyphs for each nav item rather than icon components or image assets.
standouts: The file explicitly frames emoji nav icons as a deliberate brand convention.
The active state is shown with a dashed border while keeping the same surface background.
The example covers several platform destinations, including Projects, Dashboard, Landing, Files, Gallery, Brand Kit, Calendar, Templates, Pricing, View All, FAQs, and Tutorials.
conventions: Uses the local preview-card pattern with a dsCard comment, linked _card.css, and a .card wrapper.
Keeps styling file-local in an inline style block.
Uses CSS custom properties for typography, colour, spacing-adjacent radii, and borders.
Uses semantic-light HTML structure with div-based nav examples rather than an actual nav element or links.
concerns: Emoji rendering is platform-dependent, so icon appearance may vary across operating systems and browsers.
The six-column grid is fixed in this file and has no local responsive adjustment.
notes: Brief this file as the preview card that documents and demonstrates the product sidebar's colour-emoji navigation convention.
questions: Should these nav examples be treated as visual samples only, or should the convention imply real navigation semantics elsewhere?
Is the dashed active border the intended active-state treatment for emoji nav items generally or only for this preview?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents colour emoji as a deliberate sidebar navigation brand convention and shows concrete examples.
intention_signals: It seems intended to preserve a recognizable platform-side navigation idiom by making the emoji icon choice explicit and visually reproducible in the design system.
novelty: Using colour emoji directly as navigation icons is the distinct visual move in this file.
contribution: Contributes a documented emoji-based sidebar icon convention, including example destinations, active-state treatment, and token-aligned preview styling for retaining that brand cue in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML design-system preview card documenting a colour-emoji sidebar navigation convention with a token-styled grid of labelled nav items and one active state.
intention_for_embedding: Created to communicate that platform sidebar navigation should preserve colour emoji as a deliberate brand convention.
